defends: ATT&CK mapping currency: the version pinned in mapping/ATTACK_VERSION still matches MITRE's CURRENT published Enterprise release, so the technique IDs every other check cites out of mapping/controls.json have not silently rotted (threat-intelligence currency, A.5.7 / A.8.8 / CC7.1).
extraction: Parse the source JSON. Select the collections[] entry whose name == "Enterprise ATT&CK" (NOT collections[0], NOT the global max), then take the entry in its versions[] with the greatest version compared as numeric major.minor. Compare that published version against mapping/ATTACK_VERSION.version.

statuses.fail: confirmed stale — published Enterprise > pinned (names the version gap)
statuses.unknown: couldn't verify — network error, parse miss, shape miss, source unreachable, or pin ahead of published. NEVER a silent pass.

not_a_gate: A stale result is a MAINTENANCE finding (review the changed technique IDs, re-map mapping/controls.json, bump mapping/ATTACK_VERSION) — it gates the audit's own currency, NOT a client build/deploy.
note: Fixed evidence manifest — the pin and poll source are authoritative in mapping/ATTACK_VERSION (single source of truth; the check reads them there, not from this manifest). attack:[] in mapping/controls.json is intentional: this is a meta/governance control, mapped to ISO/SOC currency controls only, with no adversary technique of its own. Fixtures carry a higher-numbered DECOY non-Enterprise collection to prove Enterprise selection over collections[0]/global-max.
